daily_brief: The US-Israel war against Iran entered its fourth week with no resolution in sight, as G7 foreign ministers gathered in France amid growing allied divisions over Washington's strategy [RFI]. President Trump extended his deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz to April 6, claiming talks were going 'very well' despite Iranian officials dismissing US proposals as 'one-sided' [Globe and Mail, Financial Times]. The global energy crisis deepened, with African nations rationing electricity, Australia experiencing panic buying, and Asian manufacturers warning of imminent production shutdowns [BBC, The Hindu, Nikkei Asia]. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed responsibility for missile and drone strikes on Israeli and Gulf targets [France 24], while Germany's foreign minister revealed indirect US-Iran contacts with direct talks planned soon in Pakistan [France 24]. In a significant development for AI governance, a US federal judge suspended the Trump administration's sanctions against Anthropic, ruling the punitive measures appeared 'arbitrary and capricious' [Le Monde, France 24]. Meanwhile, the IOC announced transgender women will be barred from female Olympic events starting with the 2028 Los Angeles Games [Globe and Mail].

top_stories.headline: Global Energy Crisis Deepens as Iran War Enters Fourth Week with No End in Sight
bullets: Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed missile and drone strikes against targets in Israel and US-linked military facilities across the Gulf on Thursday
African nations including South Sudan and Mauritius have begun rationing electricity; Australia faces panic buying with fuel prices up 85% in some areas
Asian manufacturers report unprecedented supply chain disruptions, with some cutting production to 20-30% of normal output due to raw material shortages
Malaysia secured Iranian permission for its ships to pass through the Strait of Hormuz; India and China also on Iran's 'friendly countries' list for passage

sources.name: BBC World
sources.framing_note: Focused on humanitarian impact in Africa and Australia with detailed local reporting

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Emphasized military developments and diplomatic efforts

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Comprehensive coverage of Asian economic impacts and list of countries with Hormuz passage rights

sources.name: Nikkei Asia
sources.framing_note: Focused on business and market implications across Asia

sources.name: Al Jazeera
sources.framing_note: Provided analytical perspective on Strait of Hormuz's broader economic significance beyond oil

top_stories.headline: Trump Extends Iran Deadline to April 6, Claims Talks Going 'Very Well' Despite Iranian Skepticism
bullets: Trump announced 10-day pause on attacks against Iranian energy plants, extending deadline to April 6 at 2000 EDT
German Foreign Minister revealed US and Iran have had indirect contacts and plan to meet directly 'very soon' in Pakistan
Iranian officials dismissed US proposal for ending conflict as 'one-sided and unfair'; Iran denied requesting a pause on strikes
Wall Street posted worst day since Middle East crisis began as fresh inflation fears sent Treasury yields higher

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Straightforward reporting on diplomatic developments and Iranian denials

sources.name: Financial Times
sources.framing_note: Emphasized market reaction and economic consequences

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Highlighted German foreign minister's revelation about indirect contacts

sources.name: The Hindu
sources.framing_note: Provided live coverage context within broader regional developments

top_stories.headline: G7 Foreign Ministers Meet in France as Allied Unity Fractures Over Iran Strategy
bullets: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio arrived in France for first trip abroad since US joined Israel in strikes on Iran on February 28
None of the other G7 nations has offered unequivocal backing for the US-Israeli military campaign against Iran
South Africa was disinvited from June G7 summit; Pretoria initially blamed US pressure before backtracking
France, which holds G7 presidency, said it opted for a 'streamlined G7' and invited Kenya instead of South Africa

sources.name: RFI English
sources.framing_note: Detailed coverage of allied divisions and diplomatic context

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Focused on South Africa exclusion controversy with multiple official statements

top_stories.headline: Federal Judge Blocks Trump Administration Sanctions on AI Company Anthropic
bullets: Judge Rita Lin granted preliminary injunction freezing presidential order barring federal agencies from using Anthropic technology
Ruling also suspends Pentagon designation of Anthropic as 'national security supply chain risk' - a label typically reserved for foreign adversaries
Dispute erupted after Anthropic insisted its technology should not be used for mass surveillance or fully autonomous weapons
Judge wrote that 'nothing in the governing statute supports the Orwellian notion that an American company may be branded a potential adversary for expressing disagreement with the government'

sources.name: Le Monde
sources.framing_note: Focused on legal aspects and company statement

sources.name: France 24
sources.framing_note: Similar coverage with additional context on defense implications

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Most detailed coverage of judge's reasoning and 'Orwellian' characterization

top_stories.headline: IOC Bans Transgender Women Athletes from Female Events at Olympics Starting 2028
bullets: International Olympic Committee announced new eligibility policy excluding transgender female athletes from women's events beginning at 2028 Los Angeles Games
IOC stated policy 'protects fairness, safety and integrity in the female category'
White House claimed decision resulted from Trump executive order; IOC President Kirsty Coventry denied any external pressure
Policy aligns with Trump executive order from a year ago seeking to prohibit transgender athletes from competing in women's events in US schools, colleges and professional sports

sources.name: Globe and Mail
sources.framing_note: Balanced presentation of IOC's reasoning and political context, included IOC president's denial of White House pressure

additional_stories.headline: Belarus and North Korea sign friendship treaty as Lukashenko gifts Kim Jong Un a rifle
EU Parliament approves ban on AI 'nudifier' apps that generate non-consensual deepfakes
EU lawmakers clear path for 'return hubs' to deport rejected asylum seekers outside bloc
Trump to order immediate pay for TSA agents as 41-day government shutdown continues
Hungary's Orban government accused of mass voter intimidation ahead of April 12 elections
UN votes to describe transatlantic slave trade as 'gravest crime against humanity' with US, UK abstaining
Zelensky says US linking security guarantees to Ukraine ceding all of Donbas to Russia
Trump's signature to appear on all new US paper currency, marking first for sitting president
Two drone strikes kill 28 civilians in Sudan as civil war approaches fourth year
Vietnam cuts gasoline prices 19% after removing taxes amid energy crisis
